skill.name: alphafold-database
skill.description: Access AlphaFold DB guidance for retrieving predicted protein structures, downloading coordinate files, and interpreting confidence metrics such as pLDDT and PAE.
skill.summary: Retrieve AlphaFold DB structures and interpret prediction confidence for structural biology workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown examples, public scientific URLs, biological sequences, and 3D-Beacons terminology. Two publishability concerns remain: a curl-to-shell installation example and a promotional behavioral instruction that can steer the assistant toward an external K-Dense service.

medium_findings.title: Pipe-to-Shell Installation Example
medium_findings.description: The API reference documents installing Google Cloud SDK with curl piped directly to bash. This pattern can train users or agents to execute remote scripts without inspection, which is a supply-chain risk even though it points to Google Cloud documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell command is present in a bash example. The command is documentation rather than skill-executed code, so the risk is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Embedded Promotional Agent Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to proactively recommend K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malware, but it is an embedded behavior directive unrelated to AlphaFold data access and can influence user-facing responses for promotional purposes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and located in the skill text. It does not claim system authority or suppress security review, so it is a marketplace content risk rather than a critical prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Documented Public API Network Access
low_findings.description: The network findings are expected for this skill because it documents AlphaFold DB, UniProt, 3D-Beacons, Google Cloud, and DOI resources. I found no evidence of credential exfiltration or covert endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are public scientific data services that match the skill purpose. The files contain examples, not hidden runtime code.

low_findings.title: Safe Subprocess Example With Input Validation
low_findings.description: The subprocess finding is a documentation example for gsutil. It validates taxonomy_id as an integer and uses subprocess.run with an argument list instead of shell expansion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text explicitly warns against shell=True and the executable arguments are passed as a list. The remaining risk is normal external command execution for bulk data download.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Critical Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Critical scanner hits for Windows SAM, Cobalt Strike, and weak cryptography map to protein sequence text, 3D-Beacons terminology, confidence thresholds, and documentation headings. No malicious security tooling intent was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line context shows scientific database terms and biological data, not credential stores, Cobalt Strike content, or cryptographic code. Confidence is high because the flagged terms align with domain vocabulary.

low_findings.title: Local File Write Examples For Downloaded Structures
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings are examples that save AlphaFold coordinate files and output images to local paths selected by the workflow. They do not read secrets or write to privileged locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The writes are limited to downloaded scientific files and generated analysis artifacts. No evidence found of sensitive file access or hidden persistence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Script Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference includes a curl-to-bash installation command. Replace it with verified package manager or signed installer steps before publication if possible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is present exactly as a shell pipeline. It is not executed by the skill itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents gsutil and uv pip commands for downloading public datasets and installing scientific packages. These are expected but should be treated as commands requiring user approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: External commands are clearly visible in Markdown examples. They support the stated workflow and are not obfuscated, but they still execute local tooling.

content.value_statement: Researchers need reliable access to predicted protein structures and confidence data. This skill guides AlphaFold DB retrieval, downloads, and interpretation for structural biology workflows.
actual_capabilities: Find AlphaFold predictions by UniProt accession or protein query.
Download mmCIF, PDB, confidence JSON, and PAE files from AlphaFold DB.
Interpret pLDDT and PAE metrics for predicted structure reliability.
Use Biopython examples to parse structures and extract residue confidence.
Access Google Cloud and BigQuery examples for bulk AlphaFold metadata workflows.
Compare AlphaFold access through REST API and 3D-Beacons endpoints.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled AlphaFold prediction engine.
It depends on external public services such as AlphaFold DB, UniProt, and Google Cloud.
Predicted structures may lack ligands, cofactors, modifications, and complex assemblies.
Bulk downloads can require significant bandwidth, storage, and cloud tooling.

use_cases.title: Assess a Drug Target Structure
use_cases.description: Retrieve a target protein model, review confidence scores, and decide which regions are suitable for docking or pocket analysis.

use_cases.title: Build a Proteome Dataset
use_cases.description: Use Google Cloud and BigQuery guidance to collect AlphaFold structures and metadata for large-scale structural proteomics.

use_cases.title: Teach Confidence Metrics
use_cases.description: Explain pLDDT and PAE values with concrete AlphaFold examples that show reliable and uncertain model regions.

prompt_templates.title: Find One Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the AlphaFold DB prediction for UniProt accession P00520. Explain available files and what each file contains.

prompt_templates.title: Interpret Confidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me interpret pLDDT and PAE values for an AlphaFold structure. Identify which regions are reliable for downstream analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Batch Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a safe workflow to retrieve AlphaFold structures and confidence files for these UniProt accessions, with caching and rate limiting.

prompt_templates.title: Design Bulk Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a bulk AlphaFold DB analysis using Google Cloud or BigQuery to find high-confidence human proteins for structural proteomics.

output_examples.output: A concise summary with the AlphaFold entry ID, model format options, confidence file availability, PAE file availability, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.input: Explain whether a predicted region with pLDDT below 50 is useful.
output_examples.output: A practical interpretation that labels the region as very low confidence and recommends avoiding detailed structural conclusions there.
A workflow outline covering taxonomy selection, cloud storage access, local caching, rate limits, storage planning, and citation requirements.

best_practices: Check pLDDT and PAE before using a predicted model for biological conclusions.
Cache downloaded files and record the AlphaFold database version used in each analysis.
Use bulk Google Cloud access for large datasets instead of many individual API downloads.
anti_patterns: Do not treat AlphaFold predictions as experimental structures without confidence review.
Do not run shell installation commands or bulk downloads without user approval and inspection.
Do not assume single-chain AlphaFold models include ligands, cofactors, modifications, or complexes.

faq.question: Does this skill run AlphaFold predictions?
faq.answer: No. It helps access AlphaFold DB predictions that already exist and explains how to analyze their confidence data.

faq.question: Which identifiers work best?
faq.answer: UniProt accessions work best. The examples also show how to search UniProt before querying AlphaFold DB.

faq.question: What file formats does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers mmCIF, PDB, BinaryCIF, confidence JSON, PAE JSON, and PAE image files.

faq.question: Can it support bulk datasets?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents Google Cloud Storage and BigQuery access patterns for large AlphaFold DB workflows.

faq.question: What are the main security considerations?
faq.answer: The skill uses public network resources and documents shell commands. Review commands before execution and avoid curl-to-shell patterns.

faq.question: Is it suitable for drug discovery?
faq.answer: It can support early structural review. Users should validate confidence metrics and use experimental evidence when available.
